content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660
. Fax (916) 322-0886
July 21, 1999
William G. Knight
University Counsel And Counsel for
California State University, Channel
Islands Site Authority
401 Golden Shore
Long Beach, CA 90802-42 10
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A 99-165
Dear Mr. Knight:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest code
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
Is the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority (CISA) subject to the
regulations regarding the adoption of a conflict of interest code by a state, single county or multi-
county agency?
CONCLUSION
CISA is a state agency and it must follow the provisions of Regulation 18750 in adopting
a conflict of interest code.
Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, Section 18109-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-99-165
Page 2
FACTS
You are writing in your capacity as General Counsel for the California State University
(CSU) and as counsel for CISA. The following facts were provided either in your letter or
telephone conversations on June23 or July 1, 1999.
CISA was authorized by the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority
Act (Stats. 1998, c. 861, Sections 1 and 2). The Legislature indicated in this legislation its intent
to provide a means of mitigating the harmful effects and potentially blighted conditions caused
by the closure ofthe former Camarillo State Hospital and to enhance the economic, cultural, and
social development ofthe region by facilitating the development of a state university campus and
other compatible uses on the site.
CISA is governed by a board of seven members, four members are representatives ofthe
Trustees of CSU as designated by the Chancellor of CSU; three members are representatives
from the County of Ventura, two appointed from within their own ranks by the Ventura County
Board of Supervisors and the third representative must be a city officer from a city within
Ventura County. A chairperson and a vice chairperson are elected to serve for one year.
The General Counsel of CSU is designated as the legal counsel for the authority. Upon
the approval ofthe General Counsel of CSU, the authority may employ legal counsel to carry out
its duties including, but not limited to, the employment of bond counsel in connection with the
issuance of bonds.
CISA is granted all powers reasonably necessary or proper to carry out the powers and
responsibilities expressly granted or imposed or reasonably implied under CISA, or may exercise
any power common to the county and the Trustees of CSU necessary to carry out the provisions
of CISA, and it has the power to issue bonds, notes and other debt instruments.
CISA must adopt a plan to use ad valorem property tax revenues for the benefit or
support of the development of the CSU campus on the site, including, but not limited to, the
mitigation of project-specific and cumulative onsite and offsite environmental impact attributable
to the development of the campus.
CISA will receive all of the local government share of sales and use tax revenues
pursuant to Part 1.5 of Division 2 of the Revenue and Taxation Code derived from the retail sale
or use of tangible personal property that occurs oh the site. In the 2030-2031 fiscal year and each
fiscal year thereafter, the authority will convey and transfer to the County of Ventura 50 percent
of all sales and use tax revenues derived from the retail sale or use of tangible personal property
that occurs on the site.
California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority Fund was created in the State


File No. A-99-165
Page 3
Treasury, to be administered by the authority. All capital plans for the university portion of the
site that are proposed to be funded through moneys in the fund must be included in the annual
five-year Capital Outlay Program report of the California State University that is submitted to the
Legislature and the Governor each year.
The Legislature found that the closure ofthe Camarillo State Hospital and the
development of a California State University campus on the site requires development of a reuse
plan by the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority. The reuse plan will
provide for use ofthe site and allocation oftax revenues to the authority for reuse and
development on the site with priority to the development of California State University facilities
and must include an implementation plan adopted and periodically revised pursuant to Section
33490 ofthe Health and Safety Code.
The Legislature also found that the provision of housing is itself a fundamental purpose
of CISA. There is a generally inadequate supply of decent, safe, and sanitary housing on the site
available to the faculty and staff of the California State University, Channel Islands, and to
persons and families of low or moderate income, as defined by Section 50093. The inadequate
supply of this housing threatens the accomplishment ofthe primary purposes of the authority.
Therefore, the Legislature declared that the provision of low and moderate cost housing is of
statewide benefit and ofparticular benefit and assistance to the redevelopment ofthe former
Camarillo State Hospital as the site of a California State University campus. Not less than 20
percent of all taxes that are allocated to CISA must be deposited into a separate Low and
Moderate Income Housing Fund in the State Treasury, to be administered and used for the
purposes of increasing, improving and preserving housing on the site for the faculty and staff of
the CSU, Channel Islands, and low and moderate income housing on the site available at
affordable housing cost to persons and families of low or moderate income, as defined in Health
and Safety Code Section 50093 and very low income households as defined in Section 50105.
ANALYSIS
You are requesting guidance regarding which regulation you should follow in adopting a
conflict of interest code for the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority.
There are no standards to determine whether a government agency is a local government agency
or a state agency. Regulation 18249 provides four criteria which must be met for an agency to be
considered a state agency. However, the Commission did not adopt this regulation to determine
whether an entity is a state agency for the purposes of adopting a conflict of interest code.
Instead, transcripts of the Commission’s adoption of Regulation 18249 show it was intended to
define the term “state agency” only as it is. used in Chapter 6 of the Act, dealing with lobbying.
In the absence of any other standards, we will look to 18249 for guidance in answering your
question.
Regulation 18249 provides that an agency is a state agency only if all of its four criteria


File No. A-99-165
Page 4
are met:
1.
The agency is authorized by statute, executive order or the California
Constitution.
The California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority was authorized by the
California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority Act, 1998 California Stats., Ch 861
(Senate Bill 1923). This legislation is very detailed in spelling out the responsibilities and duties
of the Authority as well as placing limits on the powers ofthe Authority.
2.
At least one voting member is an elected state officer or is appointed by an elected
officer or an state agency official or a state agency.
Four regular voting members of the Authority and two alternates are designated by the
Chancellor of the California State University.
3.
The agency is financed in part by any state funds or is subject to appropriation in
the state budget.
CISA is provided the General Counsel of the California State University as its counsel
and the services of a secretary, treasurer, controller and other staff by the California State
University. All capital plans for the university portion of the site that are proposed to be funded
through moneys in the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority Fund must be
included in the annual five-year Capital Outlay Program report ofthe California State University
that is submitted to the Legislature and the Governor each year. The Trustees of the California
State University, on behalf of the authority, must report to the Legislature and the Governor by
July 31 of each year on all expenditures made during the prior fiscal year for facilities that
increase the enrollment capacity of the site.
4.
An area larger than one county is included in its jurisdiction.
The Authority is created to facilitate and provide for the financing and support of a site to
be developed as a campus of the California State University. California State University is itself
a state agency. The authorizing legislation also states that the Legislature finds that the health,
safety and welfare of the people of California will be served by and will depend on the
development and support ofthe conversion of the former Camarillo State Hospital into a
California State University campus. The Authority is responsible for developing a campus
which, though wholly contained within Ventura County, will serve to benefit all of the citizens of
the state.
All four criteria of 18429 are met and this makes a strong case that CISA is a state
agency.


File No. A-99- 165
Page 5
There are 27 state agencies, which have conflict of interest codes, whose authority is
limited to a relatively small geographic or an area much smaller than the entire area ofthe state.
Without investigating each we think that one common element is that the purpose of each is to
serve the welfare of all of the citizens of the state of California. Each of these entities followed
the requirements of 18750 in adopting and later in amending their conflict of interest code.
Those agencies are: Area Boards on Developmental Disabilities Areas I through XIII; California
Exposition and State Fair; California Science Center; California Tahoe Conservancy; Coastal
Commission; Colorado River Board of California; Del Mar Race Track Authority; Delta
Protection Commission; Hastings College of Law; Lake County Winegrape Commission; Lodi
Woodbridge Winegrape Commission; San Francisco Bay Conservation and Development
Commission; San Joaquin River Board Conservancy; Santa Monica Mountains Conservancy;
State Coastal Conservancy.
Given that the over-arching purpose of the authority is the establishment of a new campus
to become a part of the California State University system which serves a statewide need, we
find it difficult to say that it is anything other than a state agency irrespective of other factors
which tend to mitigate against that conclusion.
The ability of the authority to: receive property taxes, local sales and use tax revenues
which are generated from sales and uses which occur on the site, and the requirement that it must
return 50 percent of all sales and use tax revenues to Ventura County, makes it unique and
interjects some factors indicating that it may not be a state agency. However, we do not think
those factors overcome a number of other factors which make it a state agency. Those factors
are: the Authority was created by the Legislature and will make regular reports to the Legislature
and the Governor; a majority of the members of the Authority are appointed by a state agency
official, the Chancellor of CSU; the resources to launch and operate the Authority are provided
by a state agency, CSU; and the essential purpose of the Authority is to create and develop a new
campus of CSU which serves to educate the citizens of the state.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
L2Lyy
By:
wayne Imberi
I’olitical Reform Consultant II

sections.question:
Is the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority (CISA) subject to the
regulations regarding the adoption of a conflict of interest code by a state, single county or multi-
county agency?
sections.conclusion:
CISA is a state agency and it must follow the provisions of Regulation 18750 in adopting
a conflict of interest code.Page 2

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the California State University, Channel Islands Site Authority (CISA) subject to the
regulations regarding the adoption of a conflict of interest code by a state, single county or multi-
county agency?

CONCLUSION: CISA is a state agency and it must follow the provisions of Regulation 18750 in adopting
a conflict of interest code.Page 2
